name: Castile Soap + ACV Hair Rinse
description: A two-step sulfate-free hair washing system using diluted castile soap for cleansing and apple cider vinegar rinse to restore pH, close the cuticle, and add shine.
shelfLife: Diluted soap: use fresh; ACV: 2+ years
effectivenessNote: Effective cleansing without sulfates. ACV rinse closes cuticle and removes buildup. 2–4 week transition period as scalp recalibrates oil production.
warnings: 2–4 week adjustment period — hair may feel oily or waxy initially
Hard water + castile soap = soap scum in hair (use soapwort instead)
Always dilute both soap and ACV
ACV rinse is essential after castile wash to restore acidic pH
scienceNote: Castile soap's alkaline pH (~9) opens hair cuticle during washing. ACV rinse (pH ~3) closes the cuticle, locking in moisture and creating the smooth surface responsible for shine. This mimics the acid-balanced formulation of commercial conditioners.
